biography.value: Thráinn Ashward is the commanding Inquisitor of the Pale Maw forces at Beinvaka. Tall and gaunt with a scar across one eye, he wears robes of deep purple with silver entropy symbols. His manner is clinical rather than fanatical — he has thought this through and is absolutely certain. He is the primary antagonist of Beats 16 and 18, commanding the forces that try to stop Kaelen’s ritual.
biography.director:
BEATS 16 & 18 — Beinvaka & Surt’s Forge

Pale Maw Inquisitor. He/him. Tall, gaunt, scar across one eye, deep purple robes. Clinical certainty — not fanaticism but absolute conviction.

BEAT 16 ULTIMATUM (opening):
“Leave the Keeper with us. You may go. We will let you pass. Or: if you refuse, we will take her — and you will all die. I am not making a threat. I am describing what will happen.”

OPTIONS THE PARTY HAS:
(a) Combat (20–30+ cultists + Inquisitor — very likely a wipe).
(b) Mad Dash Montage — flee immediately. Does not require talking to Thráinn at all.
(c) Negotiate with the Elder of Beinvaka (not with Thráinn — he will not negotiate personally).

TACTIC IN BEAT 16: Waits. Does not attack first. Will attack if any hero makes an aggressive move. Will not stop the party if they attempt the Mad Dash — he orders pursuit instead.

IF KILLED IN BEAT 16: Yrsa Frostbane takes over in Beat 18. Opening: “Thráinn died speaking the truth. I will finish what he started.”

IF ALIVE IN BEAT 18: Same stat block (see Pale Maw Inquisitor NPC monster file). Returns with 30 SP max if wounded but survived. Final words if defeated: “You delay the inevitable. The Fimbulwinter is right. The world should end.”

PALE MAW PHILOSOPHY (for social scenes): Entropy is not evil — it is correct. The Ever-Ember is not hope but defiance of natural order. Kaelen is not a hero but a heretic. “She would chain the world to warmth it no longer deserves.”
